ingredients.prep: optional for a light Creole tomato note

steps.text: Season shrimp: Pat shrimp dry. Season with a pinch of salt and black pepper. Keep chilled while you start the grits.

Cook grits: In a saucepan, bring water and stock to a boil. Whisk in grits slowly. Reduce heat to low and simmer, whisking often, until thick and tender. Add milk if using. Stir in 2 tbsp butter and optional cheddar. Keep warm on very low heat, adding a splash of water/stock if needed.

Render bacon/sausage (optional): In a skillet over medium heat, cook chopped bacon until crisp (or brown diced sausage). Transfer to a plate, leaving some fat in the pan (or add 1 tbsp butter if lean).
Cook aromatics: Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to the skillet. Cook until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.

Build quick Creole pan sauce: Stir in optional tomato paste and cook 30–60 seconds. Add Worcestershire, hot sauce (optional), thyme, paprika, cayenne, and a small splash of stock or water to loosen into a light sauce.
Cook shrimp quickly: Increase heat to medium-high. Add 1 tbsp butter, then add shrimp in a single layer. Cook just until pink and opaque, flipping once. Avoid overcooking.
Finish: Return bacon/sausage to the pan. Stir in lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and heat.
Serve: Spoon creamy grits into bowls and top with shrimp and pan sauce. Garnish with green onions and parsley.
